focus: correctness: manifest refresh and resolver gates

findingDetails.title: Concurrent refreshes can lose a generation/policy update
findingDetails.description: Refresh reads, compiles, and plain-overwrites without serialization or compare-and-swap.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Refresh implementation and adjacent tests reviewed; no refresh serialization or concurrency assertion exists.
findingDetails.claim: Concurrent refresh calls can overwrite one another after reading the same generation because refresh uses an unlocked plain overwrite.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked refresh implementation and adjacent tests for a lock, compare-and-swap, atomic replacement, or concurrent refresh test; only mint has a concurrent create-if-absent test.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Refresh may be externally serialized, but no such serialization is enforced or evidenced in this scope.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: A refresh lock or compare-and-swap protocol plus a concurrent refresh regression test proves coherent commits.

noveltyJustification: First correctness pass identified one directly evidenced replacement-concurrency risk; resolver fail-safe behavior and mint concurrency were confirmed clean.

focus: correctness: per-hub routing and parity invariants
noveltyJustification: Follow-up correctness pass confirmed scoring, ambiguity, target-resolution, and parity failure distinctions with direct implementation and regression evidence; no new finding.

focus: security: path, flag, and serving boundaries

findingDetails.title: Concurrent refreshes can lose a generation/policy update
findingDetails.description: Refresh reads, compiles, and plain-overwrites without serialization or compare-and-swap.
findingDetails.claim: Concurrent refresh calls can overwrite one another after reading the same generation because refresh uses an unlocked plain overwrite.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Replayed the iteration-1 evidence; no refresh serialization or concurrency test was found.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: External orchestration may serialize refresh, but the code does not enforce it.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: A refresh lock or compare-and-swap protocol plus a concurrency regression test proves coherent commits.

findingDetails.title: Summary-only P1 generated by incomplete carried-forward state
findingDetails.description: The reducer synthesized a summary-only finding because iteration 2 carried a cumulative P1 count without a findingDetails entry.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The finding exists only in reducer output and was not asserted in the iteration-2 narrative or delta.

noveltyJustification: Security pass found no new security issue; path, symlink, flag, environment, and fail-closed serving controls were directly verified. The prior reducer artifact was explicitly disproved and carried-forward state repaired.

reason: Reducer-only summary artifact was disproved during the security pass; it had no implementation or test evidence and was not an asserted finding in iteration 2.

findingDetails.title: Concurrent refreshes can lose a generation/policy update

findingDetails.claim: Concurrent refresh calls can overwrite one another after reading the same generation because refresh uses an unlocked plain overwrite.

findingDetails.title: Completion status conflicts with required packet gates
findingDetails.description: The packet claims complete while P1 checklist rows and operator sign-off remain open and plan/tasks/decision metadata remain planned.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All seven canonical packet documents were compared; the contradiction spans status metadata, checklist protocol, task completion, and sign-off.
findingDetails.claim: The packet's complete status violates its own P1 completion protocol because required items and sign-off remain open while canonical planning documents still say planned.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched the canonical packet for explicit operator approval or a documented P1 deferral; none was recorded.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The implementation shipped, but shipped implementation does not itself satisfy the packet's explicit evidence protocol.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: All required metadata, tasks, checklist rows, and sign-off are reconciled or a canonical operator-approved deferral is recorded.

findingDetails.title: SD-015 limitation and follow-up are stale
findingDetails.description: The summary says there is no dedicated SD-015 lock-in test, but the test suite contains a named positive clause test and an adversarial served-route counterexample.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Directly read the named test block and the stale limitation/follow-up entry.
findingDetails.claim: The SD-015 test-gap limitation is stale documentation, not missing test coverage.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: The limitation and follow-up are marked resolved with the dedicated test evidence cited.

noveltyJustification: Traceability review independently confirmed one new P1 lifecycle contradiction and one P2 stale test-gap claim. The SD-015 test gap is disproved by direct test evidence; F001 remains active.

findingDetails.title: Default-on cohort is maintained in multiple independently edited sources
findingDetails.description: The seven-hub cohort is repeated in the promoted resolver, authored resolver twin, and advisor TypeScript flag module rather than generated from one canonical source.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The same cohort literal exists in three maintained source surfaces; tests detect divergence only after an edit.
findingDetails.claim: A future cohort change can update one source while leaving another stale; current tests observe but do not prevent this drift.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked sync tooling and foundation tests for a generator or pre-write canonical source; none was found for the advisor cohort.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The current test suite catches divergence in CI, reducing impact but not removing the maintenance hazard.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: All cohort consumers derive from one source or a generation gate makes stale copies impossible.

noveltyJustification: Maintainability review found one concrete P2 synchronization hazard across runtime, authored, and advisor cohort literals. Existing tests and sync verification reduce present risk; no new P0/P1 issue was found.
